title: Aristavena setacea
intro: Aristavena setacea, also known as Pteris setacea, is a species of fern native to Brazil, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve skin health, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Aristavena setacea include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aristavena setacea is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects.
taxonomy: Aristavena setacea is a genus and species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a member of the Bromeliaceae family, which is a group of tropical plants commonly known as pineapples or air plants. The classification of Aristavena setacea places it in the order Poales. This plant is native to South America and is known for its unique appearance and growth habits. Its classification is further broken down into the family, genus, and species, with the full classification being Aristavena setacea, family Bromeliaceae.

preparations: Aristavena setacea, also known as the "Brazilian ironplume," is used in traditional herbal medicine.
